tool_args.label: Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE)
tool_args.raw_text:
One of the most impactful modern tools in that strategy is Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE). Last month, we expanded ACE to five new routes: the M2, M4, M42, M100, and Bx5. The results from ACE have been amazing. Across the city, ACE improves bus speeds by over 5% on average. On some routes, the gains are even more dramatic. Let's look at the Bx35, where ACE has increased speeds by 20%. Combined with the 181st Street busway, buses in that corridor are now moving 40% faster than they were before.

Turning to the Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE) Expansion, Crichlow reported that five new bus routes — the M2, M4, M42, M100, and Bx5 — are now ACE-enabled. He noted camera enforcement has led to average speed gains of 5%, with some corridors achieving improvements of up to 30%. He added that the program has also contributed to a reduction in collisions and emissions. Crichlow credited the strong partnership with NYC DOT and noted his shared vision with Commissioner Rodriguez.

payload.description: The official May 2025 ACE expansion added M2, M4, M42, M100, and Bx5 in two implementation-date cohorts.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Last month, we expanded ACE to five new routes: the M2, M4, M42, M100, and Bx5.
